item.title: Field Notes
item.shortDescription: A weekly-ish note sharing useful ideas, books, tools, questions, and experiments from the archive.
item.description: Field Notes is the lightweight dispatch for useful things before they become polished essays.
item.whatItIs: A short email for ideas, objects, books, questions, and experiments worth keeping.
item.whyIMadeIt: Some useful things are too small for essays but too valuable to disappear into private notes.
item.whatILearned: A newsletter needs a clear promise before it needs volume.
item.nextStep: Publish a sample issue and turn the archive page into the canonical signup surface.
item.documentaryLead: This is the lightweight publishing channel for things before they become polished essays. It should feel like a useful note from the archive, not a content machine.
item.currentQuestion: What is the smallest note format that is consistently worth opening?

proofArtifacts.label: Promise
proofArtifacts.value: One useful idea, object, book, question, or experiment from the archive.

proofArtifacts.label: Constraint
proofArtifacts.value: It has to be short enough to send and useful enough to keep.

buildLog.title: Kept the scope small
buildLog.note: The next version needs a sample issue before it needs a cadence.

item.summary: A weekly-ish note sharing useful ideas, books, tools, questions, and experiments from the archive.
